skill.name: documentation-generation-doc-generate
skill.description: Generate API documentation, architecture notes, README content, user guides, and documentation automation plans from an existing codebase.
skill.summary: Creates maintainable technical documentation from code and project structure.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, credential, and blocker patterns, but review found them inside Markdown examples and documentation templates. No prompt injection, hidden execution path, or confirmed malicious intent was found. The remaining risk is that users could copy example commands, URLs, or placeholder secrets without review.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static command findings occur in Markdown code fences, README templates, CI examples, and resource references. These samples can guide agents to propose commands, but they are not executable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged command strings are visibly inside fenced Markdown examples or references. I found no standalone script, hook, or instruction that executes them automatically.

low_findings.title: Network Calls And URLs Are Placeholder Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Network indicators appear in OpenAPI server examples, Swagger CDN references, generated client snippets, and cURL templates. They use example domains or public documentation assets, with no evidence of exfiltration logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network patterns are presented as generated documentation examples. The endpoints are placeholders and do not collect local data from the skill.

low_findings.title: Credential References Are Documentation Placeholders
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Environment variables and token references are shown as variables to document or CI configuration placeholders. They do not read real environment values or transmit secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The values are literal documentation placeholders such as JWT_SECRET, SECRET_KEY, and GitHub Actions secrets syntax. No code path accesses process environment variables.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Traversal Sample Reads Local Source Files
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE_MINOR: The documentation coverage example recursively opens Python files to inspect docstrings. This is legitimate for documentation generation, but users should scope it to trusted project paths and avoid including secrets in generated output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file traversal is real within the sample code, but it is not executed by the skill itself. The risk depends on whether a user implements the sample without path controls.

low_findings.title: Blocker Heuristics Are Not Confirmed By Semantic Review
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static blocker hits for weak cryptography and reconnaissance do not correspond to executable crypto, system discovery, or malicious behavior in the reviewed context. No prompt injection text was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation prose, placeholders, or output-format descriptions. I found no weak crypto implementation, reconnaissance command, or instruction to bypass the audit.

content.value_statement: Teams often have code that changes faster than their documentation. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create structured API docs, guides, diagrams, and automation plans from the codebase.
actual_capabilities: Plans API, architecture, code, user, developer, and reference documentation.
Generates OpenAPI-style endpoint documentation from code structure and route metadata.
Creates README sections for setup, configuration, testing, contribution, and licensing.
Drafts Mermaid architecture diagrams and component descriptions.
Provides examples for documentation CI, coverage checks, Swagger UI, and Redoc workflows.
Includes safety guidance to avoid exposing secrets, internal URLs, or sensitive data.
limitations: Requires access to code, configuration, or requirements as a source of truth.
Does not verify generated documentation against a running service by itself.
Example commands and CI snippets need review before use in a real repository.
Generated docs may need product, legal, or security review before publication.

use_cases.title: Create API Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate endpoint references, schema summaries, authentication notes, and example requests from service code.

use_cases.title: Standardize Repository Documentation
use_cases.description: Build README, setup, testing, contribution, and troubleshooting sections that follow one consistent structure.

use_cases.title: Document System Architecture
use_cases.description: Produce component descriptions, dependency notes, and Mermaid diagrams for services, data stores, and clients.

prompt_templates.title: Generate A README
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single repository overview.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to generate a README for this repository. Include setup, configuration, tests, usage, contribution notes, and known gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Document API Endpoints
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for service API documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the API source files and create endpoint documentation. Include methods, paths, parameters, responses, authentication, errors, and example requests.

prompt_templates.title: Create Architecture Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for design and dependency documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create architecture documentation for this project. Include components, data flows, dependencies, deployment assumptions, and Mermaid diagrams.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Documentation Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for maintainable documentation automation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a documentation generation workflow for this repository. Include validation, coverage checks, CI steps, publishing, and review gates for secrets.

output_examples.input: Generate documentation for a FastAPI service with user and order endpoints.
output_examples.output: A documentation plan with API reference sections, schema summaries, authentication notes, error handling guidance, and a Mermaid service diagram.

output_examples.input: Create a README and contribution guide for a Python package.
output: A README outline with installation, configuration, quick start, testing, and troubleshooting sections.
A contribution guide covering local setup, branch flow, tests, and pull request expectations.

output_examples.input: Add documentation automation for an existing repository.
output_examples.output: A CI-oriented plan for generating API docs, building a static documentation site, checking doc coverage, and reviewing sensitive content.

best_practices: Start from the current codebase and identify the target audience before writing.
Review generated examples for secrets, internal URLs, and outdated commands.
Keep generated documentation in version control with validation in CI.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated docs without checking them against source code.
Do not include real tokens, private endpoints, or production credentials in examples.
Do not use the sample automation steps without adapting paths and permissions.

faq.question: Can this skill generate API documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to extract endpoint, parameter, response, authentication, and error details from code.

faq.question: Can it create architecture diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. The playbook includes Mermaid architecture examples and component documentation patterns.

faq.question: Does it require access to source code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill works best when it can inspect code, configuration, comments, or requirements.

faq.question: Does it execute documentation commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides examples and plans. Users should review commands before running them.

faq.question: Can it help with documentation automation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for CI workflows, documentation linting, coverage checks, and static site publishing.

faq.question: How should sensitive data be handled?
faq.answer: Review all generated output and replace secrets, private URLs, and internal details with safe placeholders.
